stories.title: Who Is Shiloh Hendrix? Minnesota Woman Found Guilty in Viral Racial Slur Case
stories.summary: A Minnesota jury found Shiloh Hendrix guilty of disorderly conduct for her confrontation with a videographer at a Rochester playground, acquitting her on a separate count involving a child. The verdict came after eight hours of deliberation in a case that drew national attention after a viral video surfaced in 2025. The ruling may reignite debates over free speech and the legal boundaries of offensive language.

stories.title: Education Secretary Linda McMahon Speaks to Reporters in New Bern, North Carolina
stories.summary: US Education Secretary Linda McMahon spoke to reporters in New Bern, North Carolina, on Wednesday, where she announced a $100 million grant to support teacher training and recruitment in the state. The grant aims to address a teacher shortage in North Carolina, with the state facing a projected deficit of over 3,000 teachers by the end of the year. McMahon's visit comes as the state's education system faces significant challenges, with a growing need for qualified educators.

stories.title: The Game Takes a Bottle to the Head at 'VERZUZ' With YG
stories.summary: During Thursday’s VERZUZ showdown between YG and The Game, Compton rapper The Game downed nearly an entire bottle of tequila in one gulp. The event aired live on Apple Music and YouTube on July 23, celebrating two decades of West Coast hip-hop. Both artists traded hits and memories, ending with a tribute to the late Nipsey Hussle.

stories.title: Fred Tsui Named Executive Director of Hong Kong International Film Festival Society
stories.summary: Fred Tsui has been appointed as the new executive director of the Hong Kong International Film Festival Society, effective September 23, succeeding Albert Lee. Tsui brings over 30 years of experience in the film industry, having worked in various roles including distribution, financing, and exhibition, and has established himself as a respected international sales agency for Asian titles. He will oversee the society's initiatives, including the Hong Kong International Film Festival, and report to chair Dr. Wilfred Wong.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Burnham will spend his first day in office working from the northern branch of No 10, chairing a meeting of the National Economic Council to drive devolution and local economic growth across the UK, with a focus on addressing decades of power being concentrated in the centre. The meeting will bring together senior ministers and regional mayors to put devolution at the centre of the government's mission. Strategic decisions on devolution will shift from the Treasury and Ministry of Housing to No 10 North, marking a significant shift in the government's approach.

stories.title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot as wildfires spread
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people have been evacuated from the French tourist spot in the Landes region due to spreading wildfires, with over 23,000 forced to leave their homes, campsites, and other facilities. The fires, fueled by strong winds of up to 31mph and high temperatures, have destroyed at least 105 homes and buildings, with no reported casualties overnight. Emergency efforts are underway, with the European Union providing aid, including three planes and two helicopters, to help combat the blazes.

stories.title: Why are UK fuel prices rising again?
stories.summary: Petrol prices in the UK have risen to 156.19p a litre, while diesel costs 173.15p a litre, following a decline in early July. This increase comes despite the conflict in Iran, with prices remaining below the levels reached in the summer of 2022 following Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Fuel retailers have denied accusations of price gouging, with the official markets regulator stating it has seen no evidence of retailers changing their pricing strategies to take advantage of the crisis.

stories.title: UK mortgage rates rise to highest level for a month
stories.summary: UK mortgage rates have risen to their highest level in a month, with the average rate increasing due to renewed tensions in the Middle East and higher oil prices, which have reduced the possibility of interest rate cuts by central banks. This has led to lenders, including the five biggest High Street banks, increasing their interest rates on new fixed deals. As a result, over five million homeowners are expected to see their monthly mortgage repayments increase by the end of 2028.

stories.title: Klopp starts Germany reign by threatening to quit
stories.summary: German football coach Jurgen Klopp has started his reign by warning the media that he will quit if they invade his family's privacy. The 59-year-old, appointed on a four-year deal, made the threat during his first press conference as Germany's head coach, citing his love for the job but also his readiness to call it quits if necessary. Klopp's contract will see him through to the 2030 World Cup, co-hosted by Spain, Portugal and Morocco.

stories.title: South Africa's president wins bid to temporarily halt impeachment inquiry over 'Farmgate'
stories.summary: South Africa's President Cyril Ramaphosa has secured a temporary reprieve from an impeachment inquiry over the 'Farmgate' scandal, after a court ruled in his favour. The inquiry stems from a 2022 incident where burglars stole large sums of cash hidden in a sofa from Ramaphosa's private farm, with the president denying any wrongdoing. The case will now proceed to a separate court hearing in September, where Ramaphosa will challenge the findings of an independent panel.

stories.title: BBC Buys War Drama ‘Kabul,’ Featuring Eric Dane in One of His Last Roles
stories.summary: The BBC has acquired a six-part war drama series "Kabul" featuring Eric Dane in one of his last roles, alongside Shervin Alenabi from the series "Tehran". The show is set against the backdrop of the US troops' withdrawal from Afghanistan and the Taliban's return to Kabul, following the chaotic evacuation of diplomats, soldiers, and civilians. The series will now be available to BBC audiences worldwide.

stories.title: Boxers choose Commonwealths over Love Island chance
stories.summary: Two Northern Irish boxers, Jon McConnell and Kaci Rock, have chosen to compete in the Commonwealth Games over a chance to appear on the reality TV show Love Island. McConnell will face India's Sumit in the light-middleweight division on Monday, while Rock will compete in the welterweight division against Niue's Karen Te Ruki Pasene the following day. This decision comes after they progressed through initial auditions for Love Island before pulling out due to the timing clash with the Commonwealth Games.

stories.title: Meet the Glasgow curry shop boss representing Pakistan at bowls
stories.summary: Pakistan's representative at the bowls, Qureshi, suffered a disappointing loss in his opening match against Wales' Ross Owen, losing in straight sets 12-5 and 17-0. Despite the setback, Qureshi remains optimistic, citing the need for improvement on the carpet and joking that his poor performance may have scared off his supporters. His restaurant, however, remains a popular destination, even attracting a former world number one, Banks, who has decided to dine elsewhere due to Qureshi's recent performance.

stories.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
stories.summary: A woman who had been lifting weights for years fell into an unhealthy body image spiral after planning her wedding, leading her to obsessively track her weight and calories, and eventually develop disordered eating habits. She began weighing herself daily and restricting her food intake, only to binge when she couldn't cope anymore. This cycle of extreme dieting and guilt has left her struggling with her body image.

stories.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
stories.summary: Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has ruled out introducing rent controls in England, stating that controls implemented in some areas have not necessarily brought down rents. The Renters' Rights Act, which came into effect in May, is already having a significant impact on the market, giving renters more power and control. This decision comes as Scotland prepares to introduce new rent control legislation by 2027, allowing ministers to designate certain areas where rent increases can be capped.

stories.title: Microsoft pressures LG into killing unwanted McAfee ads
stories.summary: Microsoft has forced LG to disable unwanted McAfee trial pop-ups on Windows 11 PCs connected to LG monitors, following user complaints about large ads for McAfee antivirus and LG's own apps appearing without explicit permission. The move comes after reports of the issue dating back to 2024, when a Windows driver update would silently install LG driver updates and the LG Monitor App Installer. LG has agreed to immediately disable the McAfee pop-up from its LG Monitor App Installer.

stories.title: With Hopes High for New H.I.V. Prevention Pill, Merck Takes Steps to Ensure Access
stories.summary: Companies in Africa and India will make generic versions of the pill that could cost as little as $5 per person per year. But access to the drug and other new products in much of Latin America remains uncertain.

stories.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has imposed new tariffs on 60 countries, including the UK, China, and the European Union, with a 10% to 12.5% duty on all goods, accounting for nearly all American imports. The tariffs, aimed at addressing forced labour concerns, replace an identical levy set to expire on Friday and cover 99.4% of US imports. The move follows a US Supreme Court ruling that many previous tariffs were illegally enacted, and it is expected to escalate the ongoing trade war.

stories.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
stories.summary: US and Iran have escalated their conflict in the Gulf, with Iran launching drone strikes on US military facilities and bases across the Middle East, killing four people and injuring five in a south-west attack. The strikes come after 13 consecutive nights of American attacks on Iran, and as the US plans to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages. The situation is increasingly volatile, with reports of sirens sounding in Bahrain and Jordan, and flights suspended at a major Iraqi airport.

stories.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
stories.summary: A South Korean court has ordered SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won to pay his ex-wife Roh Soh-yeong $644 million in a divorce settlement, a significant reduction from the initial $1.38 trillion award. The couple had been married for 35 years before their marriage fell apart over Chey's infidelity. The verdict marks a major development in a highly publicized case that has captivated South Korea.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: A devastating 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years has left nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-olds without work, education, or training, with many young people in coastal towns feeling isolated and despondent due to a lack of opportunities. This dire situation has been exacerbated by decades of negative media portrayals, labeling them as "lazy" or "feral". The Guardian's Against the Tide project will continue to report on the lives of young people in coastal communities across England and Wales over the next year.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Can This New Enzyme Turn Back the Clock in Bodily Tissues?
stories.summary: In a recent study, scientists devised a way of reversing the buildup of compounds that lead to some age-related diseases.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron has been spotted at the National Trust's Holnicote Estate in Somerset, a significant sighting for UK wildlife, with the bird's presence raising hopes that conservation efforts to restore freshwater habitats are paying off. The heron's appearance comes as the estate has introduced new freshwater habitats, providing a refuge for various species including water voles, otters, and white-tailed eagles. Conservationists believe this success will help wildlife thrive, improve water quality, and reduce flood risk in the region.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: England's unique chalk streams, home to over 80% of the world's remaining habitats, are under threat from prolonged hot and dry weather, prompting the Winchester Science Centre to create a play zone themed on the local landscape to educate children about the importance of preserving these rare waterways. The centre's exhibit allows children to explore the delicate balance of water flow, temperature, and oxygen in chalk streams. The Berkshire, Buckinghamshire & Oxfordshire Wildlife Trust has thrown its support behind a new Westminster group aiming to protect these vital freshwater habitats.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found that replacing gas stoves with electric ones can lead to a significant reduction in asthma symptoms, with benefits comparable to those of certain asthma medications. The study, which began in December 2024 in Ohio, found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school among 72 participants. The researchers now plan to expand the study to further investigate the potential of gas stove replacement as a treatment for poorly controlled asthma.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.
